concept.name: Communication protocol
concept.definition: An agreed set of rules two computers follow when talking, so each one knows what kind of message to expect next and what every message means.

audience: a curious adult with no domain background
atomic_reason: The whole idea maps onto an everyday phone-call ritual: hello, identify yourself, take turns, say goodbye. One short figure showing two parties exchanging messages in an agreed order makes the mechanism click, and the only ideas it leans on, like messages, rules, and taking turns in a conversation, are common knowledge for any adult.
